title: Entrepreneurship
objective: Students will explore entrepreneurship concepts including Ethics in Business, Supply Chains, Scaling Up and more.

steps.content: Today we're going to learn about Ethics in Business. Get ready for an adventure!
Being honest, being fair, treating workers well, not harming the environment; what makes a 'good' business; whether businesses should care about more than profit
Time to try this out: Name at least two things that make a business ethical (fair wages, honest advertising, environmentally responsible)
If you heard about a company paying its workers very little to make bigger profits, could they explain why that's unfair and suggest a better approach?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Ethics in Business. Name at least two things that make a business ethical (fair wages, honest advertising, environmentally responsible) Explain why a business that only cares about profit might cause problems
Let's explore Supply Chains! Have you ever wondered about this?
Where products come from before they reach the customer; raw materials, making, transporting, and selling; the journey of a product from start to finish
Let's practice together! Trace the journey of a familiar product (e.g. chocolate bar) from raw material to shop shelf
If you asked you how a chocolate bar gets from a cocoa farm to a shop shelf, could they describe the main steps in the journey?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Supply Chains. Trace the journey of a familiar product (e.g. chocolate bar) from raw material to shop shelf Identify at least three stages in a supply chain
Have you ever thought about Scaling Up? Let's find out together!
What happens when a small business grows; making more products, hiring people, reaching more customers; the challenges and opportunities of scaling
Let's practice together! Describe what 'scaling up' means for a business
Could you explain what would need to change if their small school-fair business became so popular that lots more people wanted to buy from them?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Scaling Up: Describe what 'scaling up' means for a business Identify at least two challenges of growing a business (need more money, more people, more materials)
Today we're going to learn about Pitching an Idea. Get ready for an adventure!
Presenting a business idea to others convincingly; explaining what makes it good and why people should support it; building confidence in public speaking about ideas
Let's practice together! Deliver a 1-2 minute pitch for a business idea covering: what it is, who it helps, and why it's good
If you had to stand up in front of the class and explain a business idea in two minutes, could they do it confidently and persuasively?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Pitching an Idea: Deliver a 1-2 minute pitch for a business idea covering: what it is, who it helps, and why it's good Answer at least two questions about their idea from an audience
Let's discover the amazing world of Real Entrepreneurs!
Stories of real entrepreneurs who started young: Mikaila Ulmer (Me & the Bees), Moziah Bridges (Mo's Bows), Cory Nieves (Mr. Cory's Cookies); what makes someone an entrepreneur
Let's practice together! Retell the story of at least one real entrepreneur who started as a child
Could you tell you about a real person who started a business as a kid and explain what qualities helped them succeed?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Real Entrepreneurs: Retell the story of at least one real entrepreneur who started as a child Identify three qualities that helped that entrepreneur succeed (creativity, persistence, passion)
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Social Enterprise!
Using business skills to help others or solve social and environmental problems; making money AND making a difference; examples of social enterprises
Let's practice together! Explain what a social enterprise is in their own words (a business that helps people or the planet)
Could you come up with an idea for a business that both makes money and helps people or the environment?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Social Enterprise. Explain what a social enterprise is in their own words (a business that helps people or the planet) Give an example of a real social enterprise or charity shop
